steps.title: Read the intersection
steps.content: For a graphed system of linear equations, the solution is the point where the two lines cross. You do not need to solve equations if the graph is already shown.

steps.title: Identify the coordinates
steps.content: The two lines intersect at $x=-4$ and $y=-3$, so the solution is $(-4,-3)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A is a $y$-intercept, not the intersection.
  • Choice B has the right $y$-value but wrong $x$-value.
  • Choice C is $(-4,-3)$, the intersection point. Correct.
  • Choice D has the right $x$-value but wrong $y$-value.
That matches choice C.
